templates.name: Post Purchase Inquiries
templates.content: We don't handle post-purchase requests via text and the fastest way to get help with your order is to email us at support@wearwellow.com. Please let me know if you have any questions about our products, I'm more than happy to help out!

templates.name: Do your compression socks help with varicose veins?
templates.content:
Some of our customers have found our socks helpful for treating mild varicose veins and spider veins, but everyone's body is different, and a higher compression level may be more appropriate for your individual needs.

If you have a medical condition that requires you to wear compression socks, we recommend speaking with your medical provider to understand which compression level is right for you.

templates.name: Are your compression socks graduated compression socks?
templates.content: Yes! We offer graduated, 18-25 level compression at the ankle and throughout the calf.

templates.name: Does you offer petite or big & tall sizes?
templates.content: At this time, we only offer one length in each size, but the stretch in our socks is often able to accommodate both petite and tall frames. Also, check out our sizing guide to find your perfect fit: https://wearwellow.com/pages/whats-my-size

templates.content: Give us a follow on IG: https://www.instagram.com/wearwellow/ and FB here: https://www.facebook.com/wearwellow/

templates.content: Happy to help! I'd recommend checking out our sizing guide to find your size and perfect fit: https://wearwellow.com/pages/whats-my-size If you're still unsure, just let me know!

templates.name: Where are your items made / manufactured?
templates.content: Our socks are designed in the USA and are made in China at an Intertek WCA-certified facility.

templates.name: Will your compression socks help with cold feet?
templates.content: Yes! Our socks are soft, plush, and perfect for keeping your feet cozy no matter what the temperature is outside.

templates.name: Are your compression socks good for post-surgery recovery?
templates.content: Some of our customers have used our socks for post-surgical recovery and have found them helpful. If you are planning to have any type of foot, ankle or leg surgery, we recommend speaking with your medical provider before your procedure to determine if Wellow socks will be the best compression sock solution for you.

templates.name: Do your compression socks help with swollen feet during pregnancy?
templates.content: Yes! Our socks are great for reducing mild leg and foot swelling during pregnancy.

templates.name: How do your compression socks help runners?
templates.content: Compression socks may also help with athletic recovery by reducing the amount of lactic acid in your legs.

templates.name: Do your compression socks help with low blood pressure?
templates.content:
Our moderate, 18-25 level compression socks gently squeeze your legs to help blood pump more efficiently throughout your body. Some of our customers find our socks helpful for assisting with their low blood pressure conditions, but everyone's body is different, and a higher compression level may be more appropriate for your individual needs.

If you have a medical condition that requires you to wear compression socks, we recommend speaking with your medical provider to understand which compression level is right for you.

templates.name: Free shipping
templates.content: We offer free shipping on domestic orders over $50. Maybe you can add something else to reach the minimum?

templates.name: Do your compression socks help swollen ankles?
templates.content: Our moderate level compression socks can help with mild to moderate swelling in the ankles, legs, and feet. If you have a medical condition that requires you to wear compression socks, we recommend speaking with your medical provider to understand which compression level is right for you.

templates.name: Are your compression socks medical grade compression socks?
templates.content:
In order to qualify for the "medical grade" rating, a sock's compression level must be 30 mmHg or higher. Our compression socks fall at the 18-25 level, which is considered "moderate level" compression and sits below the medical grade rating.

Moderate level compression socks like Wellow compression socks are intended for lowering the risk for severe conditions such as Deep Vein Thrombossis (DVT), blood clots, varicose veins, and spider veins. Our socks can also be used for reducing minor swelling, recovering from hard workouts, lowering leg and foot swelling during pregnancy, helping with minor injury recovery, and reducing leg fatigue from walking, standing or sitting for long periods.

templates.name: Where do you ship from?
templates.content: Our socks ship from our warehouse in Salt Lake City, Utah and typically arrive at our customers' doorsteps within 3-5 business days.

templates.name: Do your compression socks help with arthritis pain and swelling?
templates.content:
Some of our customers have found our socks helpful for treating mild swelling associated with arthritis, but everyone's body is different, and a higher compression level may be more appropriate for your individual needs.

If you have a medical condition that requires you to wear compression socks, we recommend speaking with your medical provider to understand which compression level is right for you.

templates.content: For safety reasons, we don't recommend wearing compression socks of any kind while sleeping for long periods of time. The only exception to this is if your medical provider recommends you to do so.

templates.name: How long should I wear my compression socks?
templates.content: We designed our moderate level compression socks to be comfortable enough to wear all day long, but we recommend wearing them for a minimum of 3-5 hours to get the maximum benefit.

templates.name: Do your compression socks reduce swelling?
templates.content: Yes! Our compression socks help reduce minor swelling in the legs.

templates.name: How do your compression socks work for swelling?
templates.content: By gently squeezing your legs, our moderate level compression socks gently push blood and lymph up the legs, which results in better blood flow and reduced fluid buildup. Better blood flow and reduced fluid buildup means less swelling and reduced risk for blood clots.

templates.name: What is the compression level for Wellow Compression Socks?
templates.content: Our socks offer a moderate, 18-25 graduated compression that's intended to feel like a warm, gentle hug on your legs. Currently, this is the only compression level we offer.

templates.name: How tight should your compression socks feel on my legs?
templates.content: Our socks offer a moderate, 18-25 graduated compression that's intended to feel like a supportive, gentle hug on your legs.

templates.name: Can I pay for Wellow compression socks with my insurance?
templates.content: Currently, our compression socks cannot be paid for with insurance.

templates.name: Rewards / referral
templates.content: We have a referral program where you can invite your friends and get $15 off your next order. Plus, your friends also receive 25% off their first order. Check it out here - https://wearwellow.com/pages/share

templates.name: What materials are your compression socks made from?
templates.content: Our socks are made from 76% viscose made from bamboo fibers, 16% polyester, 6% spandex, and 2% nylon. All the materials used in our socks meet IDFL Global Recycled Standards, and our socks are produced in a facility that meets Intertek WCA ethical standards.

templates.name: Do your compression socks help with Diabetes?
templates.content:
Our compression socks offer a moderate, 18-25 level compression level that's intended to feel like a gentle hug on your legs. As a result, blood pumps more efficiently throughout your body.

We have customers with mild medical conditions who use our socks and find them helpful, but everyone's body is different, and a higher compression level may be more appropriate for your individual needs.

If you have Diabetes or any other medical condition, we recommend speaking with your medical provider before you make a purchase.

templates.name: Can I put my socks in the washing machine?
templates.content: Yes! We recommend washing them on the gentle cycle with a mild detergent. Avoid using bleach and exposing your socks to excessive heat.

templates.name: Do your compression socks help shin splints?
templates.content: Our compression socks improve blood circulation, support your veins, and reduce swelling, which can help your legs recover faster from shin splints.
